title: AC Power Factor and True Power Measurement via Current and Voltage Sensing
content: True (active) power P = V_rms × I_rms × cos(φ), where φ is the phase angle between voltage and current waveforms. Apparent power S = V_rms × I_rms. Power factor PF = P / S = cos(φ). For resistive loads PF = 1; for inductive loads (motors, transformers) PF < 1. Energy consumption E = P × t (Wh).
extended_content: Measurement method for embedded systems: (1) Voltage: ZMPT101B mains-transformer module outputs a scaled AC voltage centred at 2.5 V; MCU ADC samples at > 2 kHz for 50/60 Hz. (2) Current: SCT-013-030 split-core CT clamp outputs voltage proportional to current; burden resistor sets output level. (3) Sampling: collect synchronised N cycles (N ≥ 20) of both V and I samples; compute V_rms = √(Σv²/N), I_rms = √(Σi²/N), P = Σ(v×i)/N. Power factor PF = P / (V_rms × I_rms). Library: EmonLib (Arduino) automates this with 1480 samples per second. Calibration: ICAL = (actual current from reference meter) / (HW reading); VCAL = (actual mains voltage) / (HW reading). Safety: ZMPT101B is mains-isolated; never connect mains voltage directly to ADC pins.
formula: P [W] = V_rms × I_rms × cos(φ);  PF = P / (V_rms × I_rms)
units: P: W, S: VA, V_rms: V, I_rms: A, PF: 0–1
source: OpenEnergyMonitor EmonLib Documentation; Elektrik Mühendisleri Odası AC Power Theory; IEC 61557-12
